codeReview.summary: The fix makes the negative-path test deterministic by injecting browser availability via BrowserAvailabilityProviding and asserting .browserNotInstalled(.chrome) with a test double, removing host Chrome installation dependence while preserving launch validation and typed error behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The switcher-integration-worker skill procedure is broader than this fix workflow and does not match what was practically required; it mandates full integration-test-first and full validation stack steps that were not followed for this targeted deterministic-test fix.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/switcher-integration-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure steps 2 and 5) vs worker transcript/handoff for session 750c72f5-6e9d-4a9c-aa57-896eabe68aea showing scoped xcodebuild-only verification and direct test hardening changes.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 19f0f7b diff. Implementation satisfies the feature requirement: test_launchBrowser_reportsBrowserNotInstalled_whenChromeMissing is now environment-independent through injected browser availability and deterministic assertion on browserNotInstalled(.chrome). No blocking or non-blocking code defects found in scope.
